article_meta.title: | Updated November 25, 2025 9:00am EST Nearly 1 in 4 American households

reasoning: The model produced a structurally invalid response (code fences) and included multiple questionable bias flags and misapplied labels, while missing clearer instances elsewhere in the author/editorial prose.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged text appears to be headline/subheadline or author paraphrase; no quoted source language was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Two of three items are overreaches: calling the neutral stat headline ‘Sensationalism’ and alleging ‘Bias by Omission’ over temporal clarity are not solid author-bias patterns.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed clearer author/editorial phrasing such as promotional link lines like ‘NEW CAR PRICES SOAR TO HISTORIC HIGH’/‘MOUNTING STICKER SHOCK’ and phrasing like ‘Inflation has continued to strain the budgets of American households.’
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels are misapplied: ‘Sensationalism/Emotionalism’ for a neutral statistic and ‘Bias by Omission’ for an attributed data trend are incorrect; the ‘Negativity Bias’ item is arguable but not definitive.
